tools.name: search_robots
tools.description: Search RoboHub's catalog of commercial robots by free-text query and optional filters. Returns compact summaries with catalog URLs. Note: robots with unknown pricing are excluded when max_price_usd is set.

tools.name: get_robot
tools.description: Full catalog record for one robot by slug: description, specs, SDKs, use cases, pricing and links.

tools.name: compare_robots
tools.description: Side-by-side spec comparison of two robots by slug, plus RoboHub's editorial verdict when available.

tools.name: list_categories
tools.description: All robot categories with counts and a few example robots each. Good first call to understand catalog coverage.

tools.name: request_vendor_intros
tools.description: Submit a B2B sourcing request to RoboHub on behalf of the user. Within 48 hours RoboHub replies by email with a free shortlist of 2-4 matching robots, indicative pricing and warm vendor introductions. Vendor-neutral and free for buyers. Only call this with the user's explicit consent and their real

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_robots · Private data: get_robot, request_vendor_intros · Public sink: request_vendor_intros.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_robots · Private data: get_robot, request_vendor_intros · Public sink: request_vendor_intros

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
